requirement_summary: Connector discovery snapshots are inert evidence and cannot satisfy compiler catalog authority by themselves.
notes: 05A Arbiter verdict result_class=success accepts this current offline connector admission surface.

requirement_summary: Provider tools become model-visible only through an explicit admission manifest and policy.
notes: Admission validates selected and denied tools, expected identity, schemas, capabilities, approval policy, and hashes.

requirement_summary: Connector identity contracts are frozen, exact-match, and hash deterministically while redacting secret-looking values.
notes: Covered by identity immutability, exact expected identity, timestamp provenance, and secret rejection assertions.

requirement_summary: Discovery snapshots and raw provider-tool evidence carry deterministic hashes without becoming authoritative catalogs.
notes: Fixture hash assertions pin identity, discovery snapshot, raw provider tool, schema, and provider description hashes.

requirement_summary: Admitted connector records and lowered descriptors expose deterministic descriptor and admission-record hashes.
notes: Tests compare deterministic admitted descriptors and admission records against checked-in expected hashes.

requirement_summary: Connector input and output schemas use the accepted Millforge JSON Schema subset and fail closed for unsupported forms.
notes: Admission and compiler schema tests cover unsupported input/output schemas and diagnostic evidence.

requirement_summary: Connector descriptions are bounded and treated as untrusted provider text.
notes: Description policy, maximum description length, and secret-bearing material are covered by malformed fixture tests.

requirement_summary: Admitted connector tools declare explicit allowlisted capabilities.
notes: Non-allowlisted capabilities fail admission and accepted descriptors carry required capability tuples.

requirement_summary: Connector admission preserves side-effect and idempotency metadata through descriptor lowering.
notes: Manifest-supplied policies lower into ToolDescriptor values instead of implicit module defaults.

requirement_summary: Connector admission encodes explicit approval policies and denies forbidden tools.
notes: Denied fixture rows and admission policy matrix assertions cover forbidden and explicit approval handling.

requirement_summary: Connector diagnostics expose stable MF-C codes with deterministic redacted evidence.
notes: Malformed fixture matrix asserts stable codes rather than fragile prose.

requirement_summary: Runtime connector admission snapshots bind records by compiled descriptor identity and expose deterministic snapshot hashes.
notes: 05B Arbiter verdict result_class=success accepts the current runtime-boundary evidence.

requirement_summary: Runtime rejects missing, duplicate, stale, mutated, non-connector, or descriptor-inconsistent admission records.
notes: Snapshot construction and executor construction tests cover stale and inconsistent metadata denial.

requirement_summary: Connector descriptors require a connector broker and cannot run through generic runtime implementations.
notes: Tests prove malicious direct runtime callables are bypassed for connector descriptors.

requirement_summary: Connector model-call arguments are validated before broker entry.
notes: Invalid number-schema values deny before broker entry with no captured fake broker requests.

requirement_summary: Connector dispatch authority is runtime-owned and derived from admission snapshot plus compiled binding identity.
notes: Compiled binding and admission snapshot are checked before connector broker invocation.

requirement_summary: Broker requests contain only connector-scoped identity, provider tool, arguments, and run/stage metadata.
notes: Assertions verify request shape and absence of capability envelopes, registries, approval tokens, and mutable execution context.

requirement_summary: Offline connector runtime evidence uses the deterministic fake broker only.
notes: No network, provider credentials, connector process, or external service is required by these tests.

requirement_summary: Connector identity, discovery, raw tool, schema, description, and descriptor drift are denied before broker entry.
notes: Pre-entry drift tests cover provider evidence and descriptor/admission mismatches.

requirement_summary: Runtime denies broker entry when admitted connector capabilities drift from compiled descriptor capabilities.
notes: 05B remediation history and final Arbiter verdict accept this as closed.

requirement_summary: Runtime connector approval grants are scoped by connector, provider tool, descriptor, request, run, stage, policy, identity, and expiry.
notes: Wrong scope, missing, expired, and accepted explicit approval cases are covered.

requirement_summary: Operator-out-of-band connector approval stays pending or denied unless runtime supplies accepted evidence.
notes: Runtime approval-policy tests cover operator_out_of_band pending denial without broker entry.

requirement_summary: Connector broker outputs are schema-validated, redacted, bounded, and summarized before success.
notes: Invalid connector output, redaction, bounded summary, and output hash behavior are covered.

requirement_summary: Connector retry decisions depend on side-effect certainty and idempotency policy.
notes: Doublechecker history records the confirmed-complete retry matrix passing after 05B remediation.

requirement_summary: Connector timeout and cancellation outcomes preserve conservative side-effect and retry certainty.
notes: Tests cover pre-entry and during-broker timeout and cancellation classifications.

requirement_summary: Connector provider evidence is revalidated on each invocation.
notes: Same-stage second-invocation drift checks prove evidence is not trusted once at executor construction only.

requirement_summary: Connector provider-tool lookup is scoped by connector identity and provider tool name.
notes: Fake broker keys on connector_id plus provider_tool_name and tests cover duplicate provider names across connectors.

requirement_summary: Connector traces record binding, admission, approval, drift, broker, retry, side-effect, capability, and redaction decisions.
notes: Structured trace assertions inspect success and denial fields directly.

requirement_summary: Connector runtime boundaries do not grant queue, status, daemon, approval mutation, credential, or provider authority.
notes: Broker request shape excludes runtime authority objects and runtime-owned Millrace state.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool source manifests and declarations are frozen compile-only contracts with positive package versions.
notes: 05C Arbiter verdict result_class=success accepts the current custom-tool mini-compiler surface.

requirement_summary: Custom tools compile to descriptors and records only; no executable custom runtime is introduced.
notes: Public custom_tools exports omit executor authority and execution tests keep missing-implementation behavior closed.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool runtime_kind is closed to contract_only.
notes: Enum and policy tests reject unsupported runtime kinds.

requirement_summary: Accepted custom-tool declarations lower into generic ToolDescriptor objects and ToolCatalogSnapshot entries.
notes: Registry/catalog projection is shared with built-ins and connectors rather than a parallel custom catalog.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool compilation records serialize deterministic descriptor-linked hashes.
notes: Tests assert compilation_record_sha256 is present in JSON dumps and tamper-resistant.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool input and output schemas reuse the Millforge compiler schema subset.
notes: Malformed fixture cases cover unsupported features, bounds, references, recursion, and missing output schemas.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool source manifests cannot supply executable authority, live URLs, path traversal, or expected hash escape hatches.
notes: Executable material, unsafe paths, live URLs, and secret material fail with custom-tool diagnostics.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool capabilities and artifact policies are explicit, allowlisted, and compatible with side-effect policy.
notes: Policy matrix tests cover non-allowlisted capabilities, artifact failures, and explicit capability requirements.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool approval policies are closed and enforce explicit approval for write or artifact-producing declarations.
notes: Tests reject forbidden approval and none approval for workspace-writing custom tools.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool descriptions are operator-supplied, bounded, and rejected when they contain unsafe instructions or secrets.
notes: Diagnostic redaction and malformed description fixture cases cover this boundary.

requirement_summary: Rejected custom-tool compilation results contain no partial descriptors or records.
notes: Result contract tests reject descriptors or records on failed compilation and require parity on accepted results.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool source, descriptor, record, and diagnostic ordering are deterministic across non-semantic input order changes.
notes: Valid and reordered fixtures produce equivalent accepted result dumps and stable descriptor order.

requirement_summary: Contract-only custom-tool descriptors fail closed through the existing missing-implementation path when executed.
notes: Execution tests assert descriptor-bound missing-implementation evidence, side_effect_certainty not_attempted, trace fields, and no connector broker entry for contract-only custom tools.

requirement_summary: Custom-tool diagnostics expose MF-CT001 through MF-CT019 with bounded redacted evidence.
notes: Diagnostic taxonomy and redaction behavior are asserted directly.

requirement_summary: Built-in, admitted connector, and compiled custom-tool descriptors coexist in one generic registry path.
notes: Closure smoke registers one built-in descriptor, two admitted connector descriptors, and two compiled contract-only custom descriptors in one ToolRegistry.

requirement_summary: Mixed registry descriptors project into one compiler catalog without parallel connector or custom-tool catalogs.
notes: Closure smoke freezes the generic registry as a ToolCatalogSnapshot and resolves mixed descriptor hash records through the compiler catalog protocol.

requirement_summary: A mixed harness selects exactly built-in, admitted connector, and contract-only custom descriptors from the same catalog.
notes: Mixed harness compiles through semantic validation, lowering, and compiler service with exactly one built-in, two connector, and two custom descriptor bindings.

requirement_summary: Uncompiled, ambiguous, unadmitted, denied, drifted, approval-missing, capability-missing, invalid-output, and unsafe-retry calls fail closed.
notes: Closure smoke asserts denied connector, unadmitted connector, and uncompiled custom tool IDs are missing from the mixed catalog; runtime boundary tests cover ambiguous, drifted, approval-missing, capability-missing, invalid-output, unsafe-retry, timeout, cancellation, and contract-only custom missing-implementation denials.

requirement_summary: Spec 05 closure evidence does not depend on ignored private state as product implementation evidence.
notes: Validator rejects private ignored paths as implemented product evidence while accepting them as prerequisite authority paths. No fixture_paths are listed because this row is proven by validator source assertions and .gitignore/private-state checks rather than a row-specific fixture file.

requirement_summary: Spec 05 matrix rows keep future transport, distribution, runner, custom execution, preset, evaluation, and provider behavior outside implemented scope.
notes: Validator enforces future-behavior boundaries; README and ROADMAP keep implemented behavior narrower than future integration. No fixture_paths are listed because this row is proven by README/ROADMAP text assertions rather than a row-specific fixture file.

requirement_summary: Public package exports are limited to current offline contracts, diagnostics, compiler helpers, and generic tool surfaces.
notes: Tests assert exact connector, custom-tool, and tool package exports, top-level export uniqueness, public source modules, and private Forge exclusion. No fixture_paths are listed because this row is proven by public package export assertions rather than a row-specific fixture file.

requirement_summary: Importing public packages performs only ordinary module import work without process, network, credential, daemon, or watcher activity.
notes: A fresh subprocess audit hook imports each public package and fails on process launch, network socket creation, fork, spawn, kill, or system-command events. No fixture_paths are listed because this row is proven by a subprocess import audit rather than a row-specific fixture file.

requirement_summary: Fresh wheel and sdist package audits exclude private Millrace, ideas, ref-forge, cache, credential, and generated run state.
notes: Tests build fresh temporary wheel and sdist outputs and inspect archive names for private runtime state, caches, logs, credential-like filenames, and generated artifacts. No fixture_paths are listed because this row is proven by fresh package archive inspection rather than a row-specific fixture file.

requirement_summary: README and ROADMAP describe current Spec 05 behavior as deterministic offline closure evidence.
notes: Documentation tests assert README and ROADMAP name the offline closure surface, mixed descriptor path, deterministic matrix, and current/future boundary. No fixture_paths are listed because this row is proven by README/ROADMAP assertions rather than a row-specific fixture file.

requirement_summary: Future distribution and execution work remains outside the offline closure pass.
notes: Deferred scope remains explicit for real connector transport, marketplace installation, automatic discovery or admission, custom runtime execution, production presets, Millrace runner integration, eval workflows, and live model/backend validation. No fixture_paths are listed because this row records future-only deferred scope, not an implemented fixture-backed behavior.
deferred_scope: Future-only real connector transport, marketplace installation, automatic discovery/admission, custom runtime execution, production presets, Millrace runner integration, eval workflows, live connector execution, live provider/model/tool execution, and live model/backend validation.
